mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals expanding demand-side momentum that drives equity valuations higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with combined GDPC1/HOUST growth in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 90 signals weakening supply-side capacity utilization that reduces corporate profitability, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below 90.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 200k combined with rising labor force participation (RSAFS) indicates robust employment dynamics that drive consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show negative correlation with combined PAYEMS/RSAFS growth in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in consumer credit (RSAFS) below 380 billion signals reduced demand-side leverage that lowers equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS falls below 380.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signal a supply-side recession that drives equity market risk-off behavior, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with combined INDPRO/GDPC1 declines in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC > 0.05).
